title: Huffman File Compressor
description: Developed logic for file compression and decompression in Java using Huffman Coding Trees.
info: Built a compression and decompression pipeline in Java using Huffman coding with both count-based and tree-based header formats
Implemented a custom generic priority queue from scratch to construct the Huffman coding tree
Engineered decompression to fully reconstruct the Huffman coding tree from the compressed file's header alone, with no shared state from the compression phase

title: Command Interpreter
description: Developed a command interpreter in C for a custom assembly-like language, from tokenization to execution.
info: Built a full interpreter pipeline in C — tokenizer, recursive parser, and execution engine — supporting 20 commands across arithmetic, bitwise, memory, and control flow operations
Implemented a custom hash table and call stack from scratch to handle branch resolution and recursive function calls
Added layered error handling across lexical, syntactic, type inference, and evaluation stages, with manual memory management verified leak-free

title: Dynamic Memory Allocator
description: Built a dynamic memory allocator in C, implementing custom malloc and free functions.
info: Implemented umalloc and ufree in C using a binned explicit free list with 4 size classes, LIFO insertion, and first-fit placement

Engineered deferred coalescing to merge free blocks across the heap only when no suitable block is available, improving average space utilization by 65% and achieving a throughput exceeding 8500 operations per millisecond
Built a heap consistency checker to verify free list ordering, block alignment, absence of overlaps, and that no allocated blocks remained on the free list

title: Instruction Pipeline System Emulator
description: Simulated a five-stage pipelined processor in C, from hardware elements to hazard control and cache integration.
info: Implemented core hardware elements in C including an ALU, ripple-carry adder, and register file to emulate the computational building blocks of a CPU
Built a five-stage pipeline simulator (fetch, decode, execute, memory, writeback) for the chArm-v5 ISA, resolving data and control hazards through forwarding, stalling, and squashing
Extended the ISA with 8 additional branching and conditional instructions, and optimized an ARM64 assembly function for minimum cycle count by identifying and eliminating pipeline hazards

title: NuTradish Mobile App
description: An AI-powered meal planning app that honors your cultural heritage while meeting your personal health goals.
about: Jan-Apr 2026, Associated with UT Austin's Texas Convergent student organization
info: Co-developed as a tech member in a 7-person, cross-functional team with 3 other engineers, 2 designers, and 2 product managers

Built the React Native/Expo user profile onboarding flow — a multilingual animated welcome screen and a 6-screen profiling quiz collecting dietary goals, health conditions, allergens, caloric targets, and cuisine preferences to seed the Claude API's meal personalization context
Developed the community feed with a masonry-grid layout mixing curated and user-submitted recipe cards, featuring per-account likes and bookmarks

title: The Daily Texan Mobile App
description: Co-developed a cross-platform mobile news app for UT Austin's student newspaper.
about: Jan-Present, Associated with UT Austin's The Daily Texan organization
info: Translated Figma wireframes into production-ready screens with custom typography constants and cross-platform layout consistency
Integrate the WordPress REST API to fetch and render live articles with author, category, and date metadata
